360	\begin{lemma}\leanok
361	[L4 — Noether normalisation with clearing of denominators]
362	  \label{lem:gf_noether_clear_denominators}
363	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial}
364	  \uses{lem:noether_normalization_fg}
365	  % NOTE (resolved iter-006): the LEAN SIGNATURE block below now matches the landed
366	  % Lean decl `exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`, which RETAINS the binder
367	  % `(_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))` as
368	  % the 3rd existential (after `(_ : g ≠ 0)`, before `φ`) — required to type the
369	  % AlgHom target as an A_g-algebra. The MATH statement is unaffected.
370	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (landed target, AlgHom encoding). Write
371	  %   A_g := Localization.Away g,   B_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g).
372	  % The conclusion is:
373	  %   ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : A) (_ : g ≠ 0)
374	  %     (_ : Algebra (Localization.Away g) (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
375	  %     (φ : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)
376	  %           →ₐ[Localization.Away g] Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)),
377	  %     Function.Injective φ ∧
378	  %     (letI := φ.toAlgebra;
379	  %      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g))
380	  %                    (Localization.Away (algebraMap A B g)))
381	  % The polynomial subalgebra A_g[b_1,…,b_n] is realised as the DOMAIN
382	  % `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`, embedded into B_g by the algebra map φ; the
383	  % generators b_j are φ(X_j). Compared with an earlier draft using three anonymous
384	  % instance existentials (∃ (_ : Algebra A_g B_g) (_ : Algebra (MvPoly … A_g) B_g)
385	  % (_ : IsScalarTower …)), this form keeps only the single `Algebra A_g B_g` binder
386	  % needed to type B_g as an A_g-algebra: the single φ then carries the algebra map,
387	  % `Function.Injective φ` encodes algebraic independence of the b_j, and
388	  % `Module.Finite … over φ.toAlgebra` encodes module-finiteness of B_g over the
389	  % image polynomial subalgebra. Downstream, L5 (\cref{lem:gf_polynomial_core})
390	  % consumes a finite module over `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`; the AlgHom hands it
391	  % exactly that algebra structure via `φ.toAlgebra`, with no instance revival.
392	  % Why B_g and not B: generic flatness only needs the property after one
393	  % inversion g; the integral-dependence equations only clear denominators after
394	  % inverting g (see the SOURCE QUOTE PROOF below).
395	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, normalisation step
396	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1747-L1758).
397	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
398	  % "By Noether normalisation lemma, there exist elements $b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B$,
399	  %  such that $K\otimes_AB$ is finite over its subalgebra $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$ and
400	  %  the elements $b_1,\ldots, b_n$ are algebraically independent over $K$. [...]
401	  %  If $g\ne 0$ in $A$ is chosen to be a `common denominator' for coefficients of
402	  %  equations of integral dependence satisfied by a finite set of algebra
403	  %  generators for $K\otimes_AB$ over $K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$, we see that $B_g$ is
404	  %  finite over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$."
405	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (Noether
406	  normalisation with denominators).}
407	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain with fraction field \(K\) and \(B\) a finite-type
408	  \(A\)-algebra that is a domain with \(B_K = K \otimes_A B \ne 0\). Write
409	  \(A_g = \texttt{Localization.Away}~g\) and
410	  \(B_g = \texttt{Localization.Away (algebraMap } A\,B\,g)\) for the localisations
411	  away from \(g\) and its image. Then there exist a natural number \(n\), an element
412	  \(g \in A\) with \(g \ne 0\), and an injective \(A_g\)-algebra homomorphism
413	  \[
414	    \varphi : A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \longrightarrow B_g,
415	  \]
416	  where \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] = \texttt{MvPolynomial (Fin } n)~A_g\) is the
417	  polynomial ring on \(n\) algebraically independent variables, such that \(B_g\) is
418	  module-finite over the image polynomial subalgebra via \(\varphi\). The injectivity
419	  of \(\varphi\) records the algebraic independence of the generators
420	  \(b_j := \varphi(X_j)\), and the module-finiteness is taken over the algebra
421	  structure that \(\varphi\) puts on \(B_g\). Equivalently, the classical statement
422	  ``\(B_g\) is finite over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\)'' is encoded by the single map
423	  \(\varphi\): it both names the polynomial subalgebra (as the domain
424	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n]\)) and carries the algebra structure under which \(B_g\) is
425	  finite.
426	\end{lemma}
427	
428	\begin{proof}
429	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1747-L1758).
430	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (Noether normalisation with denominators).}
431	  Apply Noether normalisation over the field \(K\)
432	  (\cref{lem:noether_normalization_fg}) to the finite-type \(K\)-algebra
433	  \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\): there are algebraically independent
434	  \(\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n \in B_K\) with \(B_K\) module-finite over
435	  \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\). Clearing denominators, each \(\bar b_j\) is
436	  represented by some \(b_j \in B\) (writing \(1 \otimes b\) as \(b\)). Fix a finite
437	  set of algebra generators of \(B_K\) over \(K[\bar b_1, \dots, \bar b_n]\) and
438	  finitely many equations of integral dependence they satisfy; let \(g \in A\),
439	  \(g \ne 0\), be a common denominator of the (finitely many) coefficients of those
440	  equations. After inverting \(g\) all these integrality relations hold over
441	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), and the chosen generators generate \(B_g\) as a module
442	  over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); hence \(B_g\) is module-finite over
443	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). The algebra homomorphism \(\varphi\) of the statement is
444	  the \(A_g\)-algebra map \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_n] \to B_g\) sending \(X_j \mapsto b_j\);
445	  the algebraic independence of the \(b_j\) over \(A_g\) (inherited from their
446	  independence over \(K\)) makes \(\varphi\) injective, and the module-finiteness just
447	  established is exactly module-finiteness of \(B_g\) over the image of \(\varphi\).
448	\end{proof}

450	\begin{lemma}\leanok
451	[L5 — polynomial-ring core of generic freeness]
452	  \label{lem:gf_polynomial_core}
453	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial}
454	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module, lem:gf_splice_shortExact, lem:gf_torsion_base}
455	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'', proof, inductive step
456	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1760-L1772).
457	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
458	  % "Let $m$ be the generic rank of the finite module $B_g$ over the domain
459	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Then we have a short exact sequence of
460	  %  $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$-modules of the form
461	  %  $0\to A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0$ where $T$ is a finite
462	  %  torsion module over $A_g[b_1,\ldots, b_n]$. Therefore, the dimension of the
463	  %  support of $K\otimes_{A_g}T$ as a $K\otimes_{A_g}(B_g)$-module is strictly less
464	  %  than $n$. Hence by induction on $n$ (applied to the data $A_g$, $B_g$, $T$),
465	  %  there exists some $h\ne 0$ in $A$ with $T_h$ free over $A_{gh}$. Taking $f=gh$,
466	  %  the lemma follows from the above short exact sequence."
467	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness'' (polynomial-ring
468	  core of the induction).}
469	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(d \ge 0\). Let \(N\) be a finite module over
470	  the polynomial ring \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\), regarded as an \(A\)-module. Then there
471	  exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \(N_f\) is free over \(A_f\).
472	
473	  This is the genuine Mathlib-absent residue of generic freeness: the bottom of the
474	  Nitsure \S4 induction, on which the whole finite-type case rests.
475	\end{lemma}
476	
477	\begin{proof}
478	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the comment above the statement (L1760-L1772).
479	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (polynomial-ring core).}
480	  We induct on \(d\). For \(d = 0\) the ring is \(A\) itself and \(N\) is a finite
481	  \(A\)-module, so the claim is exactly the finite-module leaf
482	  \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}. For \(d \ge 1\), pass to \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
483	  set \(N_K = K \otimes_A N\). If \(N_K = 0\) the torsion base case
484	  \cref{lem:gf_torsion_base} already produces \(f \ne 0\) with \(N_f = 0\) free.
485	  Otherwise let \(m\) be the generic rank of \(N\) over the domain
486	  \(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\); choosing \(m\) elements of \(N\) whose images form a
487	  \(K(X_1, \dots, X_d)\)-basis of \(N_K\) and clearing denominators gives, after
488	  inverting some \(g \ne 0\) in \(A\), a short exact sequence of
489	  \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]\)-modules
490	  \[
491	    0 \to A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]^{\oplus m} \to N_g \to T \to 0
492	  \]
493	  with \(T\) a finite torsion \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]\)-module. Because \(T\) is
494	  torsion, its support over \(K[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) has dimension \(\le d - 1\), so
495	  \(T\) is finite over a polynomial ring in fewer variables after a further
496	  inversion; the inductive hypothesis yields \(h \ne 0\) with \(T_h\) free over
497	  \(A_{gh}\). The free middle term \(A_g[X_1, \dots, X_d]^{\oplus m}\) localises to a
498	  free \(A_{gh}\)-module by the \(d = 0\) leaf applied coordinatewise, and
499	  \cref{lem:gf_splice_shortExact} splices the two ends of the localised sequence to
500	  give \(N_f\) free over \(A_f\) with \(f := gh\).
501	\end{proof}
502	
503	\subsection{Supporting finite-module helpers}
504	\label{sec:gf_finite_helpers}
505	
506	The following two file-internal helpers in the \texttt{GenericFreeness} namespace package
507	the finite \(A\)-module leaf in the two forms (flatness and module-finiteness) that the
508	chain and the geometric globalization consume. Both are already proved axiom-clean.
509	
510	\begin{lemma}
511	
512	\leanok
513	[Flat localization away, finite module]
514	  \label{lem:gf_flat_finite}
515	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite}
516	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}
517	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain and \(M\) a finite \(A\)-module. Then there exists
518	  \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that \texttt{LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M} is
519	  \emph{flat} over \texttt{Localization.Away f}.
520	\end{lemma}
521	\begin{proof}
522	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}
523	  \leanok
524	  By \cref{lem:gf_finite_module} the localized module is free over
525	  \texttt{Localization.Away f}; free modules are flat (\texttt{Module.Flat.of\_free}).
526	\end{proof}
527	
528	\begin{lemma}
529	
530	\leanok
531	[Free localization away, module-finite variant]
532	  \label{lem:gf_free_moduleFinite}
533	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite}
534	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}
535	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a module-finite \(A\)-algebra
536	  (\texttt{[Module.Finite A B]}), and \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module
537	  (\texttt{[Module.Finite B M]}) with the compatible \(A\)-module structure via the
538	  scalar tower \(A \to B \to M\). Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such
539	  that \texttt{LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M} is free over
540	  \texttt{Localization.Away f}.
541	\end{lemma}
542	\begin{proof}
543	  \uses{lem:gf_finite_module}
544	  \leanok
545	  The \(A\)-finiteness of \(M\) is \emph{derived}, not assumed: since \(B\) is
546	  module-finite over \(A\) and \(M\) is module-finite over \(B\), composition along
547	  the scalar tower \(A \to B \to M\) makes \(M\) module-finite over \(A\) (transitivity
548	  of module-finiteness, \texttt{Module.Finite.trans}). With \(M\) now a finite
549	  \(A\)-module, \cref{lem:gf_finite_module} produces the required \(f \ne 0\) with
550	  \texttt{LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M} free over
551	  \texttt{Localization.Away f}. The lemma is therefore strictly more general than a
552	  repackaging of \cref{lem:gf_finite_module}: the finite \(A\)-algebra \(B\) and the
553	  finite \(B\)-module \(M\) are the genuine inputs, and \(A\)-finiteness is a
554	  consequence of the hypotheses rather than one of them.
555	\end{proof}
556	
557	
558	\begin{theorem}
559	
560	\leanok
561	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
562	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
563	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
564	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
565	  % NOTE (re-signed iter-002): the Lean signature `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness`
566	  % previously took `F : X.Modules` with NO coherence hypothesis, under which the
567	  % statement is FALSE (a non-coherent module sheaf need not become flat on any
568	  % non-empty open). It was re-signed in iter-002 (the decl is NOT in
569	  % archon-protected.yaml) to carry the coherence encoding below; the Lean file is
570	  % now the source of truth for the exact binders and the full flatness conclusion.
571	  %
572	  % Mathlib has no single `IsCoherent` predicate at this pin; coherence over a
573	  % locally noetherian base is encoded as the conjunction of two existing
574	  % SheafOfModules predicates on `F`:
575	  %     [F.IsQuasicoherent]   (SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent,
576	  %                            Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Quasicoherent)
577	  %     [F.IsFiniteType]      (SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType,
578	  %                            Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Generators)
579	  %
580	  % LEAN SIGNATURE HEADER (now landed in the .lean file):
581	  %   theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
582	  %       (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
583	  %       [F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType] :
584	  %       ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧ (the fibrewise flatness conclusion,
585	  %       quantified over affine opens U ≤ V of S and affine opens W ≤ p⁻¹U of X, with
586	  %       Γ(F,W) given its Γ(S,U)-module structure via p.appLE — verbatim form in the
587	  %       .lean file, the source of truth).
588	
589	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
590	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
591	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
592	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
593	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
594	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
595	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
596	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
597	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
598	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
599	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
600	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
601	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
602	
603	  The coherence hypothesis on \(\F\) is essential: without it the conclusion is
604	  false, since a non-coherent \(\OO_X\)-module need not become flat over any
605	  non-empty open of \(S\). The intended formal target therefore carries \(\F\)
606	  coherent over the locally noetherian base \(S\), which — absent a single
607	  \texttt{IsCoherent} predicate in Mathlib at this pin — is encoded as the
608	  conjunction of the two module-sheaf hypotheses \(\F\) quasi-coherent
609	  (\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent}) and \(\F\) of finite type
610	  (\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType}), together with \(p\) locally of finite type
611	  and \(S\) integral and locally noetherian.
612	\end{theorem}
613	
614	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
615	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
616	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
617	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
618	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
619	% by restricting to a non-empty
620	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
621	\begin{proof}
622	  
623	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(U_0 = \Spec A \subseteq S\), where \(A\) is a
624	  noetherian domain (\(S\) integral and locally noetherian; existence of such a \(U_0\)
625	  by \texttt{exists\_isAffineOpen\_mem\_and\_subset} applied at a point of the non-empty
626	  \(S\)). We assemble the witness \(V\) from the algebraic form on a finite affine cover.
627	
628	  \emph{Step 1 — finite affine cover above \(U_0\).} Since \(p\) is locally of finite
629	  type it is in particular quasi-compact, so the open subscheme \(X_{U_0} = p^{-1}(U_0)\)
630	  is quasi-compact; choose a finite affine cover \(\{W_j = \Spec B_j\}_{j}\) of
631	  \(X_{U_0}\) by affine opens of \(X\). Because \(p\) is locally of finite type, each
632	  ring \(B_j = \Gamma(X, W_j)\) is a finite-type \(A\)-algebra.
633	
634	  \emph{Step 2 — read off a finite module on each patch.} On each \(W_j\), quasi-coherence
635	  of \(\F\) (\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent}) identifies \(\F|_{W_j}\) with the
636	  associated sheaf \(\widetilde{M_j}\) of the section module \(M_j = \Gamma(\F, W_j)\), and
637	  finite type of \(\F\) (\texttt{SheafOfModules.IsFiniteType}) makes \(M_j\) a finite
638	  \(B_j\)-module. Through the scalar tower \(A \to B_j \to M_j\), \(M_j\) is the data fed
639	  to the algebraic form.
640	
641	  \emph{Step 3 — apply the algebraic form and take the common basic open.}
642	  \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic} applied to \((A, B_j, M_j)\) yields \(f_j \in A\),
643	  \(f_j \ne 0\), with \((M_j)_{f_j}\) free over \(A_{f_j}\). As the cover is finite, set
644	  \(f := \prod_j f_j \ne 0\) (a non-zero element of the domain \(A\)) and
645	  \(V := D(f) \subseteq \Spec A \subseteq S\), a non-empty (basic) open; over \(V\) every
646	  \((M_j)_f\) is free over \(A_f\) (freeness is preserved by the further localization
647	  \(A_{f_j} \to A_f\)).
648	
649	  \emph{Step 4 — freeness \(\Rightarrow\) flatness, checked locally.} On each patch the
650	  localized section module is free, hence flat over \(A_f\) by
651	  \texttt{Module.Flat.of\_free}; the fibrewise flatness conclusion (flatness of
652	  \(\Gamma(\F, W)\) over \(\Gamma(S, U)\) for affine \(U \le V\), \(W \le p^{-1}U\)) then
653	  follows from these local freeness statements by the localize-at-maximal-ideals
654	  flatness criterion \texttt{Module.flat\_of\_isLocalized\_maximal}. This produces the
655	  required \(V\) with \(\F|_{X_V}\) flat over \(\OO_V\).
656	\end{proof}
657	
658	\section{Mathlib status}
659	\label{sec:flatstrat_mathlib_status}
660	
661	Mathlib (master \texttt{b80f227}) provides \texttt{Module.Flat}
662	(\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic}), the module-theoretic notion of
663	flatness applicable affine-locally to the section modules of \(\F\), together
664	with the dévissage and Noether-normalisation infrastructure that the algebraic
665	generic-flatness argument consumes
666	(\texttt{IsNoetherianRing.induction\_on\_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime},
667	\texttt{Module.FinitePresentation.exists\_free\_localizedModule\_powers},
668	\texttt{exists\_finite\_inj\_algHom\_of\_fg}).  It does \emph{not} yet contain
669	the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness (a finite module over
670	\(A[X_1, \dots, X_d]\) becomes free after inverting a single element), nor the
671	geometric statement \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
672	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness}) packaging the algebraic form
673	(\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}) over a noetherian integral base.  These
674	are introduced in
675	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean}; the
676	file-internal \texttt{GenericFreeness} namespace already lands the
677	finite-module / finite-morphism special case axiom-clean.

Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
  -- The genuine proof is a strong induction on `d`; the base case is closed here
  -- and the inductive generic-rank dévissage step is the surviving residue. (We
  -- case-split rather than `induction d` because the module instances depend on
  -- `d`, so a faithful induction must universally quantify `N` over each `d`.)
  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d with hd | hd
  · -- Base case `d = 0`: `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A`, so `N` is module-finite
    -- over `A` itself (via `Module.Finite.trans` through the iso), and the claim
    -- is the finite-module leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`.
    subst hd
    haveI : Module.Finite A (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) :=
      Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv A (Fin 0)).symm.toLinearEquiv
    haveI : Module.Finite A N := Module.Finite.trans (MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A) N
    exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N
  · -- Inductive step. Pass to `K = Frac A` and split on whether `N_K = 0`.
    by_cases htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)
    · -- Torsion sub-case `N_K = 0`: closed by the L1 torsion base case applied

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean

Iter 006 — GF-alg lane. Primary mandate: deploy the CHURNING corrective —
restructure L5 `exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` to **strong induction on
`d` with `N` universally quantified**, so the IH applies to the lower-variable
torsion quotient `T`. **Status: DONE and verified clean.** Build green; sorry
count unchanged at 4 (the 4 sorries are L4@415, L5-dévissage@513, algebraic
assembly@550, geometric wrapper@612 — same set as entry, no new sorries).

## exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (L5, line ~460) — STRUCTURAL RESTRUCTURE LANDED

### Attempt 1 — strong-induction skeleton (the CHURNING corrective)
- **Approach:** Replace the flat `rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos d` skeleton (which had
  NO inductive hypothesis in scope, hence a structurally unfillable non-torsion
  branch) with `induction d using Nat.strong_induction_on generalizing N with | _ d IH`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED (skeleton lands axiom-clean; closed branches unchanged).
- **Key insight:** `generalizing N` automatically reverts `N` together with all
  five of its `d`-dependent instances (`AddCommGroup`, `Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)`,
  `Module.Finite`, `Module A`, `IsScalarTower`), producing exactly the needed IH:
  ```
  IH : ∀ m < d, ∀ (N : Type _) [AddCommGroup N] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N]
         [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N] [Module A N]
         [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin m) A) N],
         ∃ f ≠ 0, Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N)
  ```
  Verified via `lean_multi_attempt` AND `lean_goal` at the residual sorry (line 513):
  the non-torsion branch now has `IH`, `hd : d > 0`, and
  `htors : ¬Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) N)` all in scope.
- **Branches kept inside the new skeleton (both still closed):**
  - `d = 0` base → `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A N` (via the
    `MvPolynomial (Fin 0) A ≅ A` finiteness transfer — unchanged).
  - `d ≥ 1` torsion (`N_K = 0`) → `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N htors` (unchanged).

### Surviving residue — generic-rank dévissage SES (honest partial, line 513)
- **What remains:** with `N_K ≠ 0`, construct the generic-rank short exact sequence
  `0 → A_g[X]^{⊕m} → N_g → T → 0` (T torsion, support dim `< d`), Noether-normalise/reindex
  `T` onto `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A` with `m' < d`, then:
  1. `IH m' (proof m' < d) T` ⟹ `h ≠ 0` with `T_h` free over `A_h`;
  2. `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` (the d=0 leaf) coordinatewise ⟹
     the free middle `A_g[X]^{⊕m}` localises free over `A_{gh}`;
  3. `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3, proved) splices ⟹ `N_f` free,
     `f := g·h`.
- **Why Mathlib-absent:** searched `lean_leansearch`
  ("finite module over polynomial ring over noetherian domain generically free") and
  `lean_local_search "generic"` — Mathlib has `Module.freeLocus` /
  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` (the d=0 leaf, already
  used) but NO generic-rank-SES / generic-freeness-over-`A[X]` infrastructure. The two
  genuinely missing pieces are (a) the generic rank + free-submodule-with-torsion-cokernel
  SES construction, and (b) the support-dimension-drop reindex of `T` onto fewer variables.
  The full IH-application recipe is documented inline at the sorry.

## exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial (L4, line ~415) — NOT ATTEMPTED THIS ITER
- Per PROGRESS.md, L4 was "attempt only if budget remains after the L5 restructure."
  Budget was directed to landing + verifying the L5 mandate solidly. Step-1 (Noether
  normalisation over `K` via `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`) is already in the body;
  the residue is the Step-2 denominator-clearing descent (`b̄_j = 1⊗b_j`, finitely many
  integral-dependence equations, common denominator `g`, build the explicit AlgHom
  `φ : MvPolynomial (Fin s) A_g →ₐ[A_g] B_g` + module-finiteness). This is also
  genuinely Mathlib-absent (no API for clearing integral-dependence denominators into a
  single localisation). Left as the existing honest sorry — unchanged.

## Effort-break recommendation for the planner (next-iter ramp)
Both surviving residues are single monolithic Mathlib-absent constructions; recommend
splitting each into named sub-lemmas before the next GF iter (matches the queued ramp):
- **L5 dévissage** → e.g. `gf_generic_rank_ses` (constructs the SES `0 → A_g[X]^m → N_g → T → 0`),
  `gf_torsion_reindex` (re-presents the torsion `T` as finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin m') A`,
  `m' < d`), then a thin assembly that applies `IH` + L3 splice. Exact signatures depend on
  the generic-rank API one chooses (likely a hand-built `Module.rank`-over-`FractionRing`
  notion), so I did not stub them (would risk wrong types).
- **L4** → e.g. `gf_clear_one_denominator` (single integral equation) + a `Finset`-fold to a
  common `g`, then the AlgHom assembly.

## Needs blueprint entry
None — no new declarations introduced. The restructure is internal to the existing
`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial` proof body (signature untouched); all referenced
helpers (`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`, `_of_torsion`, `_of_shortExact`) already
have blueprint blocks.
